rationale.root_cause: At the bad FLAW site, memcpy uses sizeof(*structCharVoid) to copy into charFirst[16], overwriting the adjacent voidSecond/voidThird pointers on the heap. This heap-buffer-overflow corrupts voidSecond, which is later dereferenced by printLine, causing a crash.
rationale.local_fix: Change the memcpy length to sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst) so only the 16-byte field is written. This prevents overwriting the following pointer fields.
rationale.global_fix: Use a bounded field-copy helper or a typed buffer wrapper that couples the buffer with its size and enforces limits at compile time. This prevents confusing the struct size with the field size in future copies.
